value.us_asymmetry: Europe traded a normal session on Friday June 19; Juneteenth closed only US venues, so the European daily move is measured against June 19 while the US June 22 move is measured against June 18

text: European equity markets traded a normal regular session on Monday, June 22, 2026, with live closing prints and full volume on the LSEG FTSE 100 daily table (about 1.06 billion shares) and dated June 22 closes across the Trading Economics Euro Area and Germany pages. Because European venues do not observe Juneteenth, they traded a full session on Friday, June 19, so the June 22 daily moves are measured against the June 19 close, one session more recent than the US benchmark, whose June 22 move is measured against June 18.

stoxx_europe_600.basis: dpa newswire +0.58%; Trading Economics dated wrap +0.7% to about 640; vendor delta disclosed

euro_stoxx_50.basis: Trading Economics dated wrap +0.4% to 6,320; CFD-settle math implies about 6,315; single-vendor, CFD-tracked

dax.basis: Trading Economics Germany June 22 news settle 25,140 / +154 / +0.62%

ftse_100.basis: Investors Chronicle/LSEG precise historical close; dpa +0.72%

market_session: European regular close on 2026-06-22; FTSE 100 at the 16:35 BST London close

calculation.method: FTSE 100: June 22 close 10,437.85 minus June 19 close 10,363.27 equals +74.58 points (+0.72%); DAX +154 points to 25,140 (+0.62%) vs June 19; CAC 40 -21.03 to 8,400.11 (-0.25%); continental percentages corroborated by dpa and Trading Economics

text: European equities closed mostly higher on Monday, June 22, 2026. The pan-European STOXX Europe 600 ended around 640, up 0.58% on the dpa close wrap (Trading Economics put the same close near 640, +0.7%, and characterised it as a record high). The EURO STOXX 50 added about 0.4% to roughly 6,320 on the Trading Economics dated wrap (its CFD settle implies about 6,315). Germany's DAX rose 154 points (+0.62%) to 25,140, and the UK's FTSE 100 gained 74.58 points (+0.72%) to a precise 10,437.85 from Friday's 10,363.27. France's CAC 40 was the lone major decliner, off 21.03 points (-0.25%) to 8,400.11, weighed by luxury. Daily moves are measured against the June 19 close.

market_session: London regular session close, Monday 2026-06-22 (16:35 BST)

calculation.method: close 10,437.85 minus prior close 10,363.27 equals 74.58 points; 74.58 / 10,363.27 equals 0.72 percent

text: The FTSE 100 is the cycle's one precise official close: on the Investors Chronicle/LSEG historical daily table it opened 10,363.58, traded a 10,346.63 to 10,443.16 range, and closed 10,437.85 on volume of 1,061,872,669 shares, up 74.58 points (+0.72%) from Friday's 10,363.27. UK banks led the advance, with NatWest, Barclays and Lloyds each up about 4% per the dpa wrap.

subject.name: US-Iran peace progress and weak oil lift European equities

value.primary_support: positive progress in US-Iran peace negotiations and weak oil prices easing inflation concerns
value.iran_us: Iran said it made progress in the start of talks with the US over a permanent peace deal shortly after both parties signed a memorandum of understanding

text: European shares rose on Monday on positive progress in US-Iran peace negotiations and easing inflation concerns from weak oil prices, per the dpa close wrap. Trading Economics added that Iran reported progress in the start of talks with the US over a permanent peace deal shortly after both sides signed a memorandum of understanding, reinforcing the view that energy prices should stay lower and soften pro-inflationary risks. The de-escalation and lower-oil mix supported the broad indices, leaving France's luxury-heavy CAC 40 as the only major decliner.

value.rationale: eurozone sovereign yields pulled back, aiding the credit outlook

text: Financials and miners led the advance. The dpa wrap reported UK lenders NatWest, Barclays and Lloyds each up about 4%, while Trading Economics noted eurozone banks Santander, BBVA and Nordea rising around 2% as eurozone sovereign yields pulled back and improved the credit outlook; the Trading Economics Euro Stoxx Banks gauge closed up 1.00% for June 22. The single-stock percentages are approximate and carried at medium confidence.

gainers.Infineon_basis: dpa: about 5% on a humanoid-robot collaboration with VinRobotics; Trading Economics: +4.84% to 85.94 (Jun/22) and a separate news item +6.09%, framed as part of the AI-infrastructure rally

text: On single stocks, Infineon was the standout gainer, up roughly 5 to 6% - the dpa wrap tied the move to a humanoid-robot collaboration with VinRobotics, while Trading Economics logged Infineon +4.84% to 85.94 (and a DAX news item at +6.09%) as part of the AI-infrastructure rally; the differing rationales are disclosed. Other German gainers included Zalando (+4.12%), MTU Aero Engines (+1.65%) and Siemens (+1.64%). Luxury was the day's soft spot and the main drag on the CAC 40: Hermes fell about 6%, LVMH 3.59%, and EssilorLuxottica 2% to 4%, amid muted Moncler guidance and recent broker downgrades. SAP (-1.85%), Deutsche Telekom (-1.80%) and Scout24 (-1.86%) were among the DAX losers. Single-stock percentages are carried at medium confidence.
